sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_strings (id Int32, input_str String);
SELECT id, input_str, normalizeUTF8NFC(input_str) AS normalized, length(normalizeUTF8NFC(input_str)) AS normalized_length FROM test_strings WHERE normalizeUTF8NFC(input_str) LIKE '%â%';
CREATE TABLE test_join (id Int32, ref_id Int32, comparison_str String);

SELECT t1.id, t1.input_str, t2.comparison_str FROM test_strings t1 JOIN test_join t2 ON normalizeUTF8NFC(t1.input_str) LIKE normalizeUTF8NFC(t2.comparison_str) WHERE t1.id IS NOT NULL OR t2.comparison_str IS NULL;
SELECT count(*) FROM (SELECT id, input_str, normalizeUTF8NFC(input_str) AS nfc FROM test_strings GROUP BY nfc HAVING nfc IS NOT NULL) AS subquery HAVING count(nfc) > 1;
CREATE TABLE test_null_propagation (id Int32, nullable_str String);
INSERT INTO test_null_propagation VALUES (1, 'test'), (2, NULL), (3, 'sample'), (4, 'normalize â');
SELECT id, normalizeUTF8NFC(NULLIF(nullable_str, '')) AS normalized FROM test_null_propagation WHERE normalized LIKE 'test%';
CREATE TABLE boundary_tests (id Int32, boundary_str String);
INSERT INTO boundary_tests VALUES (1, ''), (2, 'a' + repeat('b', 1000)), (3, 'a' + repeat('ç', 500)), (4, normalizeUTF8NFC('â'));
SELECT id, length(boundary_str), normalizeUTF8NFC(boundary_str) AS normalized FROM boundary_tests WHERE length(normalized) BETWEEN 1 AND 1000 AND normalized LIKE 'a%';
